playlists.title: Quant HM Interview Prep
playlists.tagline: What Susquehanna-style hiring managers actually test
playlists.description: Research hygiene, adversarial stats, production thinking, and HM behavioral rounds — the signal prop shops and systematic funds hire on (not LeetCode trivia).

episodes.title: Research hygiene
episodes.problemYouSolve: Prove you won't ship look-ahead bias into production
sampleQuestions: Walk me through auditing a backtest for look-ahead bias.
What failure modes do you check before trusting Sharpe 2?
How do you design point-in-time feature pipelines?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Hypotheses → data lineage → as-of joins → train/test honesty → paper-trading gates. Name specific red flags (survivorship, corporate actions, snooping).
episodes.coachTip: SIG-style interviewers push on *process*, not gotcha math.

episodes.title: Probability from first principles
episodes.problemYouSolve: Show you derive claims — not quote formulas
sampleQuestions: Expected rolls until a six — derive it.
When do you distrust Monte Carlo?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Define random variable → compute expectation step-by-step → sanity-check with simulation → state variance of estimator.
episodes.coachTip: They care whether you think clearly when stuck — talk through structure.

episodes.title: Backtest → live production
episodes.problemYouSolve: Answer 'will this work at size?' not just 'did it backtest?'
sampleQuestions: Signal works at $10M — how do you scale to $500M?
Live PnL diverges from backtest — first hour checklist?
What makes you kill a strategy after launch?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: Capacity model → cost/impact → crowding → monitoring → pre-defined kill rules. Separate bug from alpha decay.
episodes.coachTip: Theorem/ex-SIG HMs often owned PnL — speak their language on risk.

episodes.title: HM behavioral: intellectual honesty
episodes.problemYouSolve: Pass the 'would I trust this person with capital?' filter
sampleQuestions: Tell me about a time you were wrong in research.
Disagreement with a senior researcher — how did it play out?
What questions do you have for me about how we evaluate researchers?
episodes.strongAnswerFramework: STAR with a real mistake → what metric broke → process fix left behind. Questions show you evaluate *their* research culture.
episodes.coachTip: At HM stage they already like your skills — they're buying judgment and temperament.
